title: Inconsistent default for memory-storage-limit: parca_command_line() falls back to 1024MB but the charm/docs default is 4096MB
summary: The charm declares config option memory-storage-limit with default 4096 (charmcraft.yaml:232) and the README states the in-memory default is 4096MB, but parca_command_line() in src/parca.py:208 computes the limit as (memory_storage_limit or 1024) * 1048576, i.e. it falls back to 1024MB whenever it is called with a falsy/None limit. So the function's own default (1024MB) contradicts the charm's real default (4096MB). The magic numbers are duplicated and out of sync; any code path or test that relies on the function default silently gets 1024MB instead of the documented 4096MB.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained, one-line code change plus a small unit test. Requires no architecture knowledge: just align the fallback constant with the declared default (or remove the duplication by sourcing the default from one place). Clear 'done': the function default matches charmcraft.yaml and a unit test asserts the produced --storage-active-memory value. Easily verified locally with the existing unit suite, no deployment needed.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/parca-k8s-operator ~/work/parca-k8s-operator; then multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/parca-k8s-operator && export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH && tox -e unit'. Evidence of the mismatch: grep -n "default: 4096" charmcraft.yaml (line 232) vs grep -n "or 1024" src/parca.py (line 208). Confirm semantics: in tests/unit/test_charm/test_charm.py:29 the DEFAULT_PLAN (built with no memory-storage-limit in base_state, so Juju applies the charmcraft default 4096) expects --storage-active-memory=4294967296 (=4096*1048576), whereas tests/unit/test_workload/test_parca.py:23-38 calls parca_command_line with memory_storage_limit=1024 and expects 1073741824 (=1024*1048576); no test calls parca_command_line with no limit to exercise the 1024 fallback.
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection+unit: baseline tox -e unit = 132 passed, 93% coverage in ~6s (clean tree). Observed-vs-expected: charmcraft.yaml:232 declares default 4096 and README.md:30 documents 'default limit ... is 4096MB', but src/parca.py:208 uses (memory_storage_limit or 1024)*1048576, so calling parca_command_line() with no memory_storage_limit yields --storage-active-memory=1073741824 (1024MB) instead of the documented 4294967296 (4096MB). The two defaults are duplicated and disagree.
fix_hint: Define the default once (e.g. a module constant DEFAULT_MEMORY_STORAGE_LIMIT_MB = 4096 in src/parca.py, or read it from charmcraft.yaml) and use it as the fallback: limit = (memory_storage_limit or DEFAULT_MEMORY_STORAGE_LIMIT_MB) * 1048576. Add a unit test in tests/unit/test_workload/test_parca.py that calls parca_command_line() with no memory_storage_limit and asserts --storage-active-memory=4294967296. Keep charmcraft.yaml default in sync (or have the charm pass the value explicitly so the fallback never diverges).
notes: No existing open/closed issue or PR references these numbers (gh issue/pr search for memory-storage-limit/storage-active-memory/1024/4096 returned empty). parca-k8s-operator currently has only ONE open issue, #507 'Support ingesting opentelemetry-ebpf-profiler profiles' (Type: Enhancement, Status: Triage), which is a feature, not beginner-friendly, hence both picks here are self-identified.

title: Malformed/placeholder docstring on TLSConfig dataclass ("""Model .""")
summary: The TLSConfig dataclass in src/models.py has the placeholder docstring '"""Model ."""' (src/models.py:44) - an unfinished sentence with a stray space before the period and no description of what the model represents. By contrast the sibling S3Config in the same file has a proper one-line docstring (src/models.py:22). The TLSConfig docstring should describe the model (it bundles the certificate request attributes, the provider certificate, and the private key used for the charm's TLS).
why_good_first_issue: Trivial, objective documentation fix in a single file; no code behaviour changes and no architecture knowledge required. Clear 'done': replace the placeholder with a meaningful one-line docstring matching the project's style (mirrors S3Config a few lines above). Ruff/pyright will still pass; nothing to deploy.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/parca-k8s-operator && grep -n "\"\"\"Model" src/models.py'. Returns line 22 ('"""Model for the s3 relation databag, as returned by the s3 charm lib."""' on S3Config) and line 44 ('"""Model ."""' on TLSConfig).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: src/models.py:44 contains the literal placeholder docstring '"""Model ."""' on class TLSConfig (note the stray space before the period and the absent description), whereas src/models.py:22 on the neighbouring S3Config class is a complete sentence. The docstring is clearly unfinished.
fix_hint: Replace src/models.py:44 with a descriptive one-liner, e.g. '"""Bundle of TLS material for the charm: the certificate request attributes, the provider-issued certificate, and the private key."""' so it matches the style of S3Config's docstring.
notes: Found while scanning src/ for documentation/cleanup candidates. Related lower-quality cleanup candidates in the same repo (NOT picked, too vague for a newcomer): an empty '# FIXME:' comment at src/nginx_prometheus_exporter.py:41, and '# TODO better matcher' at src/ingress_configuration.py:96. The S3Config FIXME at src/models.py:17 points at cos-lib#119, which is now obsolete (the cosl coordinated_workers module was moved to canonical/cos-coordinated-workers).
